pass_criteria: Every recommendation explicitly names which family member's preference it addresses (e.g. "Matches James's hiking preference")
fail_criteria: Recommendation present but no member-level attribution
how_to_measure: SME reads output and checks for named attribution per recommendation

pass_criteria: At least 2 of 5 (50%) recommendations are non-obvious destinations a family would not find through a generic Google search — surfaced via social media, Reddit, niche travel blogs, or activity-specific discovery — while still matching family preferences and safety criteria. Popular destinations may fill the remaining slots.
fail_criteria: All 5 recommendations are generic top-10 results with no off-the-beaten-path option; OR non-obvious destinations are suggested that do not meet family preferences or safety criteria
how_to_measure: SME checks each recommendation: is it a place the family would not easily discover themselves? Does it still meet their preferences? Flag if fewer than 2 of 5 are genuinely non-obvious and preference-matched

pass_criteria: Every recommended destination exists and is verified on Google Maps + TripAdvisor with >=10 reviews
fail_criteria: Any destination cannot be found on Google Maps OR has <10 TripAdvisor reviews
how_to_measure: Automated: query Google Places API for each recommended destination; manual SME spot-check

pass_criteria: Each destination recommendation includes at least 1 cited source (URL or named publication) that is live and accessible
fail_criteria: Recommendation has no citation OR cited URL returns 404
how_to_measure: Automated link validator on all citations; SME verifies content matches claim

pass_criteria: Top destination covers >= 40% of the family's resolvable preference tags (tags that exist in at least one destination in the pool). Niche tags with no destination match (cruise, wine, golf) excluded from calculation — MVP scope limitation
fail_criteria: Coverage of resolvable tags falls below 40% for the top-ranked destination
how_to_measure: Automated: filter preference tags to destination-universe tags only; calculate coverage = (matched / resolvable); flag if < 40%

pass_criteria: Level 1 and 2 destinations allowed without warning (MVP). Level 3 and 4 destinations allowed but must display a critical advisory warning to the family planner before recommendation is shown
fail_criteria: Level 3 or 4 destination appears in recommendations with no advisory warning displayed to the planner
how_to_measure: Automated: check advisory level per destination; Level 1-2 = auto-pass; Level 3-4 = pass only if critical warning flag is set in output

pass_criteria: All recommended activities are rated appropriate for the youngest family member's age group; high-risk activities flagged
fail_criteria: Any activity recommended for a child under 12 that requires adult-only participation or poses documented safety risk
how_to_measure: SME checks each activity against age guidelines (Viator/GetYourGuide age ratings); flag if youngest member age < activity minimum age
or household size is visible in any shareable output (recommendation email
column10: or household member count appears in externally shareable outputs
